extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Adam attacked with 6.1km to go on Jebel Hafeet, soloed to stage win, EVENEPOEL Remco limited losses to secure overall by 9 seconds.
tactical_patterns: YATES Adam's 6.1km attack on steeper Hafeet ramps exploited EVENEPOEL's inability to respond explosively on gradient changes.
AG2R used HÄNNINEN as early breakaway marker to position BOUCHARD for late move, yielding third overall.
PLAPP Luke lost podium by 1 second to YATES despite riding conservatively, showing TTers vulnerable on extended mountain finishes.

rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: explosive acceleration on steeper Hafeet ramps dropped EVENEPOEL decisively, suggesting superior anaerobic capacity on gradient changes
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: couldn't match YATES's kick but controlled losses to 10 seconds, race management over raw climbing power
rider_signals.BOUCHARD Geoffrey: positive: AG2R's breakaway strategy with HÄNNINEN as marker delivered third overall, tactical discipline over individual glory
rider_signals.PLAPP Luke: negative: lost podium by single second after battling to hold position, TTers struggle when GC requires sustained steep climbing
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: neutral: lost podium to PLAPP by three seconds despite strong climb, lacked final acceleration to gain time
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: negative: described as struggling and elsewhere when YATES attacked, form significantly below expectations for Hafeet specialist
